Petition To Amend Letters Of Administration From Limited Authority To Full Authoritiy Under The Independent Administration Of Estates Act
Off Calendar Apr-02-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Endy Ukoha-Ajike for Troylene Trammell (Administrator)

The Court stated that the Administrator is able to sell the property with limited authority, but she needs to petition the court for approval. Mr. Ukoha-Ajike stated there is difficulty in this case due to family history, that there is an 86 year old uncle/co-owner who is ill and owns the majority of property, and expressed concern stating that the uncle may object to the petition for approval of sale. Mr. Ukoha-Ajike also stated the parties agreed to sell the property and the siblings have filed waivers of bond, and that he is concerned because his client may not qualify for a bond. In addition Mr. Ukoha-Ajike stated the property is worth about $780,000 and that the estate gets 25%. The Court required $200,000 bond (Probate Examiner to review amount of bond). The Petition to Amend Letters of Administration for Full Authority is Granted Subject to filing of bond.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le, Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jun-12-2017 Continued To Sep-18-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Endy Ukoha-Ajike (by CourtCall) for petitioner Troylene Trammell.

Mr. Ukoha-Ajike reported that the underlying litigation has been resolved, and that he is waiting per the parties' agreement for the property to be listed and sold. The Court reminded him that the petitioner either needed to file a petition to close the estate or a petition for approval of status report. Mr. Ukoha-Ajike replied that there is no money in the estate currently, and it will get funds when the property is sold. He requested that the status hearing be continued for 90 days. The matter was continued to September 18, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Court Examiner: Sandra Hilton. Bailiff: Deputy Jaime. (Not reported)

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Endy Ukoha-Ajike appeared by telephone for Troylene Trammell (Petitioner).

Mr. Ajike stated that the Estate owns 31% of a piece of real property, that the rest is owned by a Trust, that he has negotiated with the other side to let them list the property, that they have to abide by the rules, but that they are not having the Court confirm the sale. The Court informed Counsel that he needs to file a Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration. Status on close of the Estate is continued to June 12, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Oct-19-2016 Continued To Jan-09-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Troylene Trammell in Propria Persona.

Prior to the appearance of Ms. Trammell the Court stated that Ms. Trammell had appeared on 10/17/16, was told to come back on 10/19/16 but is not here, that an Order to Appear will be issued to Ms. Trammell for 11/21/16. The Order to Appear was removed with the appearance of Ms. Trammell.

Ms. Trammell reported that she cannot afford counsel, that she liquidated her annuity to care for her mother, that the attorney's are requesting $3000.00 up front, that she and her siblings have called their uncle regarding their inheritance but he does not respond, that they want someone to make him do right, that she is also caring for her disabled sister and that the uncle has the sister's inheritance as well. The Court asked for clarification as to if there is no attorney who will take this case. Ms. Trammell stated that she previously had an attorney but found that he was working on behalf of her uncle, that she has seen 5 attorneys, that they do not want to take the case on contingency and that she contacted the paralegals on the Court's list but misunderstood what their role would be and was asking them to represent her. The Court stated that it needs a petition for final distribution for closure of the case, that it can be done by either an attorney or paralegal and the Court will provide her with the list again. The status on close of estate was continued to January 9, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court stated that the petition must be filed by this date and that it is the final part to close the case.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Sep-26-2016 Continued To Oct-19-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jul-25-2016 Continued To Sep-26-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Endy Ukoha-Ajike (via court call) for Troylene Trammell who was present in court.

The Court stated that we need a petition for final distribution, that Mr. Ajike has substituted out of the case and asked Mr. Ajike if he could assist Ms. Trammell with the petition. Mr. Ajike replied no, that Ms. Trammell says that she has no confidence in him. The Court instructed Mr. Ajike to file a Motion to Withdraw as Counsel and asked Ms. Trammell how soon she can get new counsel. Ms. Trammell stated that she let go of Mr. Ajike in February, that she has gone over the rules of court and knows she needs counsel, that she has been trying to obtain counsel with no success, that she tried to file a letter to the court but it was not accepted, that she does not understand the paperwork. The Court stated that it will provide Ms. Trammell with a list of Probate Paralegals (said list was handed to Ms. Trammell in court) and continued the status on close of estate to September 26, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the expectation that Ms. Trammell will have counsel in place and that the motion to withdraw will be filed.

Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

The Court stated that it will look for the motion to withdraw and that it will issue an order on the reconsideration of fee waiver.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

The hearing on reconsideration of fee waiver was continued to March 28, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of payment.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jan-22-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Endy Ukoha-Ajike for (petioner) Troylene Trammell.

The court stated that we are here for a petition for letters of administration and that there is an issue regarding property, rent for said property and a bond. Mr. Ukoha-Ajike reported that the property is not occupied by family members, the property is held in the name of the trust of the brother of the decedent, that rent is being paid but not going to the estate and that it is not known how much rent is being paid. The court stated that a bond would be needed to cover the value of the rent. Mr. Ukoha-Ajike stated that all siblings indicated they would sign waivers of bond but now one sibling is not responding, the property is shared with the brother of the decedent and the estate owns 31%, there is an 850 petition regarding the property to be heard on March 2, 2015, there are no known objections to this petition, and suggests a minimum bond in the amount of $20,000.00 to be increased if needed. The Court granted the petition for letters of administration with limited authority and bond in the amount of $20,000.00 and requested that Mr. Ukoha-Ajike submit an amended form of the order. Status of filing an inventory and appraisal is set for May 26, 2015 at 9:00 a.m. in dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Loos. (Not Reported)
